_meta.purpose: PROPOSED section-level patterns for a transactional purchase/booking flow. NOT yet established. Each pattern is a `plan: null` STUB pending 1:1 capture from a real Figma checkout reference. `level` says whether the pattern IS a full Section or is CONTENT to nest inside a titled Section.
_meta.arrangementGuidelines: Arrangement (horizontal vs. vertical) follows context/layout-guidelines.md → 'Reihenfolgen'; grouping follows context/gestaltgesetze.md (Ähnlichkeit, Nähe, gemeinsame Regionen, Prägnanz). Summary: equal/comparable items → horizontal; sequential/prioritized content → vertical; responsive fallback to vertical when width is tight or readability suffers.
_meta.source: TO CAPTURE — Figma file uI6oKmJ0qDhsEuE0nFah2W, checkout reference screen (node id to be recorded).
_meta.placeholderConvention: <...> = fill with real content; optional trailing '?' means the node may be dropped when empty.
_meta.refConvention: { "$ref": "block.id" } expands to that block's `plan` fragment from checkout/blocks.json. Only valid once plan is captured.

checkout.stepper.purpose: Full-width read-only step indicator as the first section. Blocked on the Stepper/Progress component gap (PROPOSAL.md #1).

checkout.option-list.purpose: 2–6 selectable, equivalent fare/option cards bound to ONE Radio group. Nest inside a titled Section (title = h2). Equal-item-action.
checkout.option-list.arrangement: The options are EQUIVALENT and COMPARABLE, so arrange them HORIZONTALLY (a Grid of equal-height cards) per layout-guidelines.md → 'horizontal, wenn Inhalte parallel erfasst / verglichen / als gleichwertig wahrgenommen werden'. Identical card styling expresses Gestalt-Ähnlichkeit; the Card region expresses gemeinsame Regionen; equal heights come free from a Cards-only Grid (equalHeights). Switch to a VERTICAL stack (ContainerVertical) when there are many options (>4), when each row carries long descriptions, or responsively when the width is too narrow for readable columns.

checkout.form-fieldset.purpose: One or more titled fieldsets of form fields. Nest inside a titled Section. For a step that shows a live summary, place this in the left column of a two-column Grid with checkout.summary in the aside (confirm Grid layout against the reference).
checkout.form-fieldset.arrangement: Fields build on each other and have a clear reading order → arrange them VERTICALLY (layout-guidelines.md → 'vertikal, wenn Inhalte in klarer Reihenfolge / aufeinander aufbauend'). Only pair fields HORIZONTALLY when they are equivalent and short (e.g. Vorname/Nachname, PLZ/Ort), keeping the reading order unambiguous; collapse pairs to vertical responsively when the width is tight.

checkout.summary.purpose: The order-summary card. As an aside beside the form, or full-width on a final review step.
checkout.summary.arrangement: As a form aside, place it HORIZONTALLY next to the fieldset (high information density, stays visible while filling — layout-guidelines.md). Keep it in its own Card so Gestalt-Nähe + gemeinsame Regionen bind the price breakdown into one unit, clearly separated from the form. On a final full-width review step, arrange the summary vertically above the action bar.

checkout.notice.purpose: A single contextual Notification for the step (info / warning / error). Drop when no message applies.

checkout.action-bar.purpose: Full-width closing action bar: secondary 'Zurück' + single Brand forward/pay action.
